children.text: Representations of sponsorship by United States or agency thereof
It shall be unlawful for any person registered under

children.tail: to represent or imply in any manner whatsoever that such person has been sponsored, recommended, or approved, or that his abilities or qualifications have in any respect been passed upon by the United States or any agency or any officer thereof.

children.text: Statement of registration under Securities Exchange Act of 1934 provisions
No provision of subsection (a) shall be construed to prohibit a statement that a person is registered under this subchapter or under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 [

children.tail: et seq.], if such statement is true in fact and if the effect of such registration is not misrepresented.

children.text: Use of name “investment counsel” as descriptive of business
It shall be unlawful for any person registered under

children.tail: to represent that he is an investment counsel or to use the name “investment counsel” as descriptive of his business unless (1) his or its principal business consists of acting as investment adviser, and (2) a substantial part of his or its business consists of rendering investment supervisory services.

children.text: Use of indirect means to do prohibited act

It shall be unlawful for any person indirectly, or through or by any other person, to do any act or thing which it would be unlawful for such person to do directly under the provisions of this subchapter or any rule or regulation thereunder.
The Securities Exchange Act of 1934, referred to in subsec. (b), is

children.tail: , which is classified principally to chapter 2B (§ 78a et seq.) of this title. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see
, substituted “General prohibitions” for “Unlawful representations” in section catchline.

, authorized representation as an investment counsel if person’s principal business consisted of acting as investment adviser, and a substantial part of the business was rendering investment supervisory services, and struck out the requirements that the person be primarily engaged in rendering investment supervisory services, or that his registration application state that the person is, or is about to become engaged primarily in rendering investment advisory services.

children.text: For transfer of functions of Securities and Exchange Commission, with certain exceptions, to Chairman of such Commission, see Reorg. Plan No. 10 of 1950, §§ 1, 2, eff.
